Interface BattleListener

public interface BattleListener
Listener interface used by BattleEngine to communicate with the UI layer.

This interface allows the engine to notify external systems (typically the GUI) about important battle events such as stage changes, log messages, and model updates.

Typical usage:

  • The UI implements this interface
  • The UI registers itself using BattleEngine.setListener()
  • The engine calls these methods whenever the battle state changes

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    onLog(String message)
    Called when the engine emits a log message.
    void
    onModelUpdated()
    Called when the underlying battle model has been updated.
    void
    onStateChanged(BattleStage stage)
    Called when the battle stage changes.

  • Method Details

    • onStateChanged

      void onStateChanged(BattleStage stage)
      Called when the battle stage changes.

      This allows the UI to react to transitions such as:

      • Hero selecting a skill
      • Choosing a target
      • Monster turn starting
      • Victory or defeat
      Parameters:
      stage - the new BattleStage representing the current phase of battle

    • onLog

      void onLog(String message)
      Called when the engine emits a log message.

      This is typically used to display messages in the UI battle log, such as skill usage, cooldown warnings, or combat events.

      Parameters:
      message - text message describing the event

    • onModelUpdated

      void onModelUpdated()
      Called when the underlying battle model has been updated.

      The UI should refresh displayed information such as:

      • Hero HP
      • Monster HP
      • Shields or buffs
      • Cooldown indicators
